display_name: ADMISSION-GATED v1 (2026-07-14 SOL admission mine). Thesis: the SOL bleed is a never-green ENTRY problem (59% of fleet entries peak<2% then bleed to the -9.68% floor; exits are TAPPED OUT — sol_bail is the only clean exit lever). Held-out admission mine (train Jul9 n=2159 + today n=80, both regimes) found the ONE robust entry edge = LIQUIDITY + DEMAND-DOMINANCE (NOT pool-age; RH's aged thesis does NOT transfer — it inverts on SOL's faster lifecycle). Gate: liquidity_usd>=34k (the only consistent single predictor, sep 0.19 both windows) AND buy_pressure_60s>=0.6 (buyers dominate the last 60s). On the UNGATED population this cuts never-green ~59->45% and lifts win ~21->29% (the edge is marginal on already-gated bots like pump_dip, large on the raw firehose). Structure cloned from baseline_v1 (unconditional, the -$1456 hole) with ONLY the admission gate added, so this IS the controlled test: does the admission gate rescue the broad entry population? PAPER. base $20. Grade forward at n>=30 vs the fleet baseline (fidelity-honest realized NG + win + net). exits inherit the fleet ladder + enforced sol_bail. Refine thresholds on REALIZED data only, never re-tune on these windows.
